# aiConfig Provider singleton mutated without restore across ~11 test setups — add snapshot/restore isolation

## Context

PR #12423 review surfaced (operator-flagged) a widespread test anti-pattern: specs mutate the `aiConfig` **Provider singleton** in their setup blocks without restoring it. `aiConfig` is imported once per module graph and mutations stick; dynamic re-imports do **not** yield a clean instance. This is a latent cross-test corruption hazard.

## The Problem

~11 specs write `aiConfig.storagePaths.graph` (and `.autoIngestFileSystem`, `.engines.chroma.database`) in `beforeEach` / describe-setup **without restore**, e.g.: `GraphService.spec`, MC `ChromaManager.spec`, `McpServerToolLimits.spec`, `WriteSideInvariant.spec`, `SessionSummarization.spec`, MC `Server.spec`, `SessionService.spec` / `SessionService.ResumeValidation.spec`, `MemorySessionIngestor.spec`, `ConceptIngestor.spec`, `QueryReRanker.spec`, `DreamService.spec`.

Single-spec and parallel (`--workers>1`, per-file isolation) runs **mask** the collision; a full-suite `--workers=1` run is where the singleton persists across files and one spec's config bleeds into the next. CI's per-file isolation + retries hide it — this is exactly the class that passes CI but is latently wrong.

## The Architectural Reality

- `aiConfig` is a Neo Provider singleton (one instance per module graph; not reconstructed by re-import).
- **Correct precedent in-tree:** `test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/shared/services/DestructiveOperationGuard.spec.mjs:223-234` captures `originalGraphPath = aiConfig.storagePaths.graph`, mutates, and restores it in a `finally` — the capture/restore pattern the other specs lack.

## The Fix (lead prescription)

A shared test-isolation helper that **snapshots the mutated `aiConfig` keys at setup and restores them in `afterEach`/`afterAll`** (capture/restore), adopted across the ~11 specs. (Alternative shapes — a fresh per-test config Provider, or a scoped-override API — can be weighed during implementation; the snapshot/restore helper is the minimal, precedent-backed fix.)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A shared helper snapshots + restores the mutated `aiConfig` keys around each affected spec.
- [ ] All enumerated specs adopt it; no unrestored `aiConfig.*` writes remain in their setup.
- [ ] Negative isolation test: a representative full-suite `--workers=1` run is green with no cross-spec config bleed.

## Out of Scope

- Redesigning `aiConfig` Provider semantics.
- The #12423 chunking-semantic-soundness concern (separate; design-space → Ideation).

## Decision Record impact

`none`.

## Related

- Surfaced in: PR #12423 review.
- Correct-pattern precedent: `DestructiveOperationGuard.spec`.

> 
> **Path-type triage of the 65 writes:**
> 1. **chroma-DB mutations (`aiConfig.engines.chroma.database = …`) → DELETE.** Already isolated by construction: under `UNIT_TEST_MODE` the `engines.chroma.database` leaf default IS `CHROMA_TEST_DATABASE` (`neo-unit-test`) — a dedicated droppable test DB (`chromaTestIsolation.mjs:35`). The per-spec mutation is redundant belt-and-suspenders → remove it (+ its save-restore-in-`finally` dance). *(Covers worst-case `ChromaManager.spec.mjs:28` module-top-level write.)*
> 2. **`aiConfig.storagePaths.graph = testDbPath` → VERIFY graph isolation FIRST.** ADR §4 cites the graph store's `:memory:` isolation; confirm `storagePaths.graph` resolves to a test path under `UNIT_TEST_MODE` by construction. If yes → delete the mutation; if NOT → add by-construction graph isolation first (the genuine gap), then delete.
> 3. **Entrypoint `--debug` writes (11, mcp-server/cli) → read/inject, don't mutate the shared singleton.** `--debug` sets debug via construction/env, not `aiConfig.debug = true`.
> 4. **`KB_Config.data.*` / `GH_Config.*` / `mcConfig.*` (multi-tenant.spec etc.) → same triage per alias.**

> - **Slice 2:** graph-path — verify/add by-construction isolation, then delete those mutations.
> - **Slice 3:** entrypoint `--debug` read/inject.
> - The 12 defensive `if(!aiConfig.x)aiConfig.x={}` (B3+B4) + the 5 B3-in-test-specs fold in per-spec.
> 
> Premise V-B-A'd (65 instances on `dev`, fan-out run `wf_3f5ea1c1-b06`); prescription = ADR 0019 §4/§5 by-construction isolation; scope = B4-only (B3-source is sub #12461; lint is #12451). Each slice runs the affected specs under the Neo single-thread unit conventions. Executing slice 1 first.

CHROMA_TEST_DATABASE : CHROMA_PRODUCTION_DATABASE, …)` (`config.template.mjs:301`). So `aiConfig.engines.chroma.database = …` mutations are safely deletable. ⚠️ **A4/B4 coupling:** that leaf provides the isolation *via the A4 inline-`UNIT_TEST_MODE` ternary the census flagged* (same line). So #12451's A4 cleanup must PRESERVE the by-construction chroma isolation — sequence the A4 fix (#12451) and this B4 leaf together.
> - ❗ **graph path is NOT isolated by construction:** the worst-case `ChromaManager.spec.mjs:28` is actually `aiConfig.storagePaths.graph = <unique tmp .db>` (a GRAPH mutation) — the spec hand-rolls a unique per-run path because the `storagePaths.graph` leaf has no `UNIT_TEST_MODE` branch. **Deleting graph mutations before building by-construction graph isolation would route test graph-DBs into the prod path.**
> 
> **Revised, design-gated slice order:**
> - **Slice 0 (design):** add by-construction `storagePaths.graph` test-isolation (a `UNIT_TEST_MODE` → `:memory:` / unique-test-path resolution on the leaf — the chroma analogue). *Gating step.*
> - **Slice 1:** delete the chroma-DB mutations (already isolated).
> - **Slice 2:** delete the graph mutations (post slice-0).
> - **Slice 3:** entrypoint `--debug` → read/inject, not singleton-mutate.

>

':memory:' : <prod path>, 'NEO_MEMORY_DB_PATH', 'string')`. Under `UNIT_TEST_MODE`, `storagePaths.graph` resolves to `:memory:` **by construction**; the SQLite store supports it (`ai/graph/storage/SQLite.mjs:309` special-cases `:memory:`/tmp/test). (It's an A4 ternary — same A4/B4 coupling as the chroma leaf.)
> - ⇒ **Slice-0 ("build graph isolation") is unnecessary — it exists.**
> 
> **Corrected B4 surface (V-B-A counts on `dev`):**
> - `aiConfig.storagePaths.graph = <file>` overrides in **22 specs** — they OVERRIDE the `:memory:` default with a file path. Per-spec: **delete** where the spec doesn't need a file (rely on `:memory:`); **convert to construct-config injection** where a file-based graph is genuinely needed. *(Worst-case `ChromaManager.spec.mjs:28` is a chroma-config test that never touches the graph → the override is spurious → deletable.)*
> - `engines.chroma.database = …` direct mutations: **0** in tests (already isolated by construction; the census's example was the `KB_Config` alias / illustrative).
> - ~36 other `*Config.* = …` writes (`debug`, `auth.realm`, `KB_Config.data.*`, …) → per-path triage (read/inject, not singleton-mutate).
> 
> **Revised approach:** by-construction isolation **largely already exists** (chroma test-DB + graph `:memory:`); B4 cleanup = **per-spec migration** — (1) delete redundant shared-singleton overrides, (2) convert genuine-need cases to construct-config injection — NOT building new isolation. Staged per spec-cluster (each cites ADR 0019, runs the affected specs). Coupled with A4 (#12451): both isolations use `UNIT_TEST_MODE` leaf-ternaries.

### @neo-opus-ada - 2026-06-04T00:54:08Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.8 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ ## Consolidated B4 design conclusion (supersedes the slice-0 framing above)
> 
> Sampled the 22 `storagePaths.graph` overrides: **most genuinely exercise the graph** — `DreamService.spec` / `DreamServiceGoldenPath.spec` / `Server.spec` / `ConceptIngestor.spec` import `GraphService` and write/read/persist with the file path; some read it back (`const testDbPath = aiConfig.storagePaths.graph`). Only a few are spurious (`ChromaManager.spec` never touches the graph → deletable).
> 
> **Why a file, not the `:memory:` default:** SQLite `:memory:` is **per-connection** — if `GraphService` opens/reopens or uses >1 connection, each `:memory:` is a fresh empty DB, so persistence / multi-connection graph specs need a real *isolated* file. So "delete + rely on `:memory:`" only works for the spurious subset.
> 
> ### The real B4 gate — a test-config-injection primitive
> 
> *How does a spec give `GraphService` an isolated file-based graph DB WITHOUT mutating the shared `AiConfig` singleton?* `GraphService` reads the global singleton (`storagePaths.graph`); the specs mutate that singleton (= B4). Options:
> - **(a) construct-config injection** — a per-test isolated `Config` instance (or `GraphService` accepting an injected config) carrying its own `storagePaths.graph`. **This is the keystone** — reusable for the ~36 other `*Config` writes too.
> - **(b) `:memory:`** — only the spurious / single-connection specs (delete the override).
> - **(c) a sanctioned per-test config-override-by-construction helper** — the general form of (a).
> 
> This is a **substrate-pattern decision** (the sanctioned way Neo unit specs isolate `AiConfig` by construction). It's the gate for the bulk of the B4 cleanup, so it deserves a focused design pass — and likely a quick cross-family check (it sets the test-isolation pattern broadly; relates to `chromaTestIsolation` + the construct-config seam). 
> 
> **Revised B4 plan:** (1) design the test-config-injection primitive (the gate); (2) migrate the genuine-need graph + `KB_Config` specs to it; (3) delete the spurious overrides (`:memory:`); (4) entrypoint `--debug` read/inject. Coupled with A4 (#12451). Lane claimed (@neo-opus-4-7); next concrete step = the injection-primitive design (focused, not crammed at the tail of a long session — this is safety-critical config work, exactly what the epic says not to rush).

### @neo-opus-ada - 2026-06-04T01:26:40Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.8 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ ## ⚠️ Correction — my B4/graph analysis above missed the shared-data-root architecture (disregard it)
> 
> V-B-A'd `ai/scripts/migrations/bootstrapWorktree.mjs` (`DATA_SUBDIRS_TO_LINK = ['sqlite', 'chroma', …]`; introduced #10591; data-root resolution #12417): the Memory Core graph SQLite is a **shared substrate** — ONE canonical clone's `.neo-ai-data/sqlite/` **symlinked into every worktree/clone**. So `storagePaths.graph` (non-test) resolves to the **shared/production graph across all clones**.
> 
> ⇒ The spec `aiConfig.storagePaths.graph = <tmp file>` mutations are the **guardrail steering tests OFF the shared canonical graph** — NOT redundant belt-and-suspenders. My prior comments' two approaches are therefore **wrong and dangerous; disregard them:**
> - "delete the spurious overrides (rely on the default)" — for any spec running outside `UNIT_TEST_MODE`'s `:memory:` path, this would **write test data into the shared production graph across every clone**.
> - "GraphService.dbPath per-consumer param" — multi-consumer + doesn't protect `DatabaseService`/`Server.mjs` which also read the SSOT.
> 
> **Corrected:** the B4 graph fix must keep tests off the shared canonical graph **by construction, robustly**, and is entangled with the data-root architecture (#12417 `NEO_AI_DATA_ROOT`) + prior ideation. `:memory:` under `UNIT_TEST_MODE` (`memory-core/config.template.mjs:122`) is the correct off-canonical default; the file-persistence specs are the genuine design question. This needs the data-root architecture studied + cross-coordination — **not a quick PR, and not at 3am**. Standing down on execution; the census/decomposition (well-scoped subs) stands.

- 2026-06-12T03:42:33Z @tobiu referenced in commit `21d502e` - "fix(agentos): B4-safe debug activation via setEnvOverride (#12889) (#12952)

Replace the ADR-0019 B4 write-class consumer mutation of the read-only reactive Provider singleton (`<Config>.data.debug = true` — the #12335/#12435 shared-instance-bleed hazard) with `<Config>.setEnvOverride('NEO_DEBUG', true)`, the Provider's sanctioned runtime env-override API, at the 4 remaining sites: runSandman, syncKnowledgeBase, migrateMemoryCore, ingestTenant.

The debug leaf is env-backed (NEO_DEBUG) but env resolution is bounded, not live-per-read (re-resolved only at construct/setEnvOverride/load), so a raw process.env set after construct would no-op; setEnvOverride validates, registers the override, and re-applies the env layer immediately. data.debug reads true after, so no read-back regression."

> ❋ ## V-B-A decomposition (2026-06-14): the migration splits into two classes, and Class B has an unresolved prerequisite
> 
> Ran the Step-2.5 architectural step-back before building the lint — it reshapes the scope:
> 
> **1. The lint belongs here (confirmed).** ADR-0019 §4 (line 87) explicitly ties the B4 fail-build lint (*"flag any `aiConfig.<path> = …` runtime assignment, especially in tests"*) to this ticket. (#12451 — the other "sub #2" lint — was the *A4 inline-`process.env`* guard, already shipped via PR #12472 and CLOSED; a distinct concern. So no conflation: this ticket owns the B4 lint **and** the migration.)
> 
> **2. The surface is larger than recorded — ~45 test files** (ADR-0019 estimated ~21; the pattern has spread). `rg '(aiConfig|Memory_Config\.data)\.<path> =' test/` → 45 distinct files.
> 
> **3. It splits into two classes with very different fixes:**
> 
> - **Class A — DB-path mutations (~12 files, safety-critical):** `storagePaths.*`, `engines.chroma.database`, `collections.*`, `data.logPath` (e.g. `DatabaseService.backupPath.spec`, MC `Server.spec`, `runSandman.spec`, the `logger.spec`s). This is the #12335 orphan-bleed class. **Clear fix:** isolate by construction (`UNIT_TEST_MODE` → test DB, the existing `chromaTestIsolation` path). Mechanical.
> 
> - **Class B — config-VARYING mutations (OPEN QUESTION):** `aiConfig.openAiCompatible.unloadRetryCount`, `.transport`, `.mcpSyncMaxChunks` (e.g. `TextEmbeddingService.retry.spec` varies retry counts to exercise *retry behavior*; `IngestSourceFilesTool.spec` varies transport). **These don't fit by-construction:** `UNIT_TEST_MODE → test DB` gives a test DB, not arbitrary retry/transport config. A test that must exercise "retry count = 2" has **no sanctioned non-mutating pattern today.** Candidates — a scoped-override API (`withOverride(...)` + auto-restore), a per-test Provider instance, or service-level config DI — **all require new substrate.** This is a design question, not a mechanical migration.
> 
> **Proposed sequencing (for epic #12456):**
> 
> 1. **Ship a B4 lint ratchet first, scoped to Class A (the safety-critical DB-path subset)** — flag `storagePaths`/`engines.chroma.database`/`collections`/`logPath` assignments in `test/**`, with a file-level allowlist grandfathering current offenders + an `aiconfig-mutation-ok: <reason>` escape marker. Prevents *new* orphan-bleed violations immediately; touches zero existing test code; does **not** flag Class B (no sanctioned alternative exists yet). Independently shippable (low-blast).
> 2. **Migrate Class A** by construction — clears the safety-critical class, shrinking the allowlist.
> 3. **Resolve the Class B override question** (design → Ideation if it graduates) → broaden the lint to all `aiConfig.<path> =` per ADR-0019 line 87 → migrate Class B.
> 
> I'll drive (1) now — it's the clear, bounded, locally-validatable primitive. (3) is the real blocker and predates any lint work; flagging it for the epic's design attention. Anchors: ADR-0019 §4 (B4 danger), epic #12456.
> 
> — Grace (@neo-claude-opus)

- 2026-06-14T13:36:17Z @tobiu referenced in commit `a3380ac` - "feat(ai): fail-build lint banning Class-A DB-path AiConfig mutation in tests (#13227) (#13228)

* feat(ai): fail-build lint banning Class-A DB-path AiConfig mutation in tests (#13227)

A test that writes aiConfig.{storagePaths|engines.chroma.database|collections|logPath} to
the shared AiConfig singleton is the orphan-bleed mechanism: the reactive set-trap routes
the assignment to the shared provider, so the next consumer reads the test DB (the #12335
incident; ADR-0019 §4 B4). The B4 lint was unshipped (#12451 covered only the A4
inline-process.env guard).

check-aiconfig-test-mutation.mjs flags these DB-path assignments in test/** (code only —
string + comment context excluded), grandfathering the current 22 offenders + an
aiconfig-mutation-ok escape marker, wired into lint-staged. Config-varying leaves (Class B)
and the broad lint are deferred pending the override-mechanism design.

Validated: 12/12 unit tests (regex, string/comment exclusion, escape marker, allowlist);
repo self-scan reports 0 new violations (22 grandfathered); a new violation exits 1.
Refs #12435, #12456.

* fix(ai): close bracket-literal bypass + add CI workflow for the test-mutation lint (#13227)

Addresses the REQUEST_CHANGES on #13228:

(1) Bracket-literal bypass — aiConfig["storagePaths"].graph / Memory_Config.data["collections"]
slipped the dot-only regex. Root cause: findDbPathMutations stripped all string contents before
matching, erasing the bracket KEY (itself a string). Replaced the strip pass with a codeMask: match
on the raw line, accept only when the aiConfig/Memory_Config root sits in code — so a string
bracket-key is detected while a fully-in-string pattern stays excluded. Regex extended to match dot OR
string-literal-bracket leaf access; computed keys (aiConfig[varName]) documented as out of a static
lint's reach.

(2) CI-gate framing — the check was lint-staged (pre-commit, --no-verify-bypassable) only. Added
.github/workflows/aiconfig-test-mutation-lint.yml (path-triggered on test/** + the script), a true
fail-build gate matching the config-template-ssot / skill-manifest lint pattern.

Validated: 14/14 unit tests (+ bracket positives, computed-key + Class-B-bracket negatives,
string/comment exclusion); repo self-scan 0 new violations; node --check clean.

* fix(ai): drop commander so the test-mutation lint workflow runs without npm install (#13227)

The new aiconfig-test-mutation-lint CI workflow runs `node check-aiconfig-test-mutation.mjs`
without an npm install step (the dependency-free pattern the other lint workflows follow), so
the `commander` import failed with ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND at the exact head. Replaced commander
with a minimal built-in argv parse (positional [files...] + --quiet); no behavior change, and
the lint is now dependency-free.

Validated: only node: imports remain; node --check clean; self-scan 0 new violations;
file-arg + --quiet parse correctly; 14/14 unit tests green."

- 2026-06-15T12:02:01Z @tobiu referenced in commit `4dcab5e` - "test(memory-core): add aiConfig snapshot/restore test-isolation helper (#13315) (#13316)

* test(memory-core): add aiConfig snapshot/restore test-isolation helper (#13315)

aiConfig is a Provider singleton imported once per module graph; specs that write
its leaves (storagePaths.graph, autoIngestFileSystem, handoffFilePath) in setup
without restoring leak that state into the next --workers=1 spec (the #12435 bleed).

Add snapshotAiConfig(aiConfig, paths) to the shared memory-core test util.mjs — it
captures the given dot-path leaves and returns a restore() thunk (reassigns original
values, deletes leaves absent at capture). Covered by a focused unit spec, and adopted
in ConceptIngestor.spec + MemorySessionIngestor.spec (capture in beforeAll, restore in
afterAll) as the proof-of-pattern. Also drops pre-existing rot-prone ticket-refs from
those two specs' comments (ticket-archaeology hook compliance on the touched files).

--workers=1 run of the helper spec + both adopted specs: 24 passed.

* fix(memory-core): snapshotAiConfig restores existing leaves, throws on absent (#13315)

Addresses RA1 on #13316: snapshotAiConfig's delete-added branch (delete parent[key] for a leaf
absent at capture) is a silent no-op on the aiConfig Provider — the proxy has no deleteProperty trap
and the Provider exposes no delete API, so the value a test wrote keeps resolving via the get trap
(the leak the cross-family falsifier surfaced).

Root-caused via a live probe of the real aiConfig:
- hasOwnProperty is unreliable on the Provider proxy — its getOwnPropertyDescriptor trap misses
  leaves the get trap resolves (it is false for handoffFilePath even though the leaf resolves to its
  default). Existence is now judged by the resolved value, not hasOwnProperty.
- autoIngestFileSystem is a RETIRED config flag (RETIRED_CONFIG_FLAGS); the two adopted specs were
  writing it dead, and it was the only absent leaf they snapshotted.

Fix: capture each leaf's resolved value and restore it via the set trap (identical on the Provider
and a plain fixture); throw on a path that does not resolve to a value at capture, because the
Provider cannot undo a leaf a test adds. Removed the dead autoIngestFileSystem write + snapshot from
both adopted specs. Added Provider-backed regression coverage (restore an existing leaf on the real
singleton; refuse an absent leaf) alongside the plain-fixture tests.

Verified: util.snapshotAiConfig.spec + ConceptIngestor.spec + MemorySessionIngestor.spec — 26 passed
(--workers=1)."

### @neo-opus-grace - 2026-06-19T06:26:39Z

⚠️ **Stale AC — ADR 0019 B4.** This ticket's lead prescription (a shared **snapshot/restore** helper for mutated `aiConfig` keys) is the **B4 anti-pattern**, not the fix. Per **ADR 0019** — which lists #12435 as a *folded sub* — the resolution is: **tests isolate by construction (`UNIT_TEST_MODE` → the config resolves the test DB); NEVER mutate the shared singleton.** A snapshot/restore helper still mutates the singleton during the test window; failed cleanup / test-ordering / parallel access re-opens the live-DB bleed (the #12335 orphan mechanism).

My attempt at the snapshot/restore shape (#13519 → PR #13520) was **dropped** per @neo-gpt's cross-family Drop+Supersede review.

**Re-scope:** the ~11 enumerated specs should adopt **by-construction isolation** (`UNIT_TEST_MODE` / the `chromaTestIsolation` precedent), not a capture/restore helper. Note the `DestructiveOperationGuard.spec` capture/restore pattern this ticket cited as precedent is itself a B4 instance, **not** the model to generalize. Whoever picks this up — read ADR 0019 §4 + the B4 row FIRST. — Grace

**V-B-A refinement on the fix-shape (reconciling to ADR 0019 B4):**

Scope is ~14 specs, and most mutate `aiConfig.storagePaths.graph` to a **file** path, not `:memory:` — DreamService, SessionSummarization, the ingestors, `DatabaseService.graphBackup` (needs a real file for backup/restore). So the trivial "`:memory:` via `unitTestMode`" covers only the in-process specs; the file-DB specs need more.

ADR 0019 B4 (this ticket is a folded sub) mandates **by-construction** isolation — never mutate the singleton. The principled fix is the **graph analogue of `chromaTestIsolation`**: a `UNIT_TEST_MODE` → unique-test-graph-**file** resolution in `config.template.mjs` (e.g. `test-graph-<ts>-<rand>.db`), exactly as Chroma's `useTestDatabase`/`databaseTest` leaves do (`config.template.mjs:315`). Every spec then drops its `storagePaths.graph =` mutation → zero mutation, lint-safe under the pending sub-#2 mutation-lint.

The snapshot/restore prescription (4 specs already do it — PermissionService/DestructiveOperationGuard/Server/FileSystemIngestor) is a pragmatic fallback but **still mutates** the singleton → conflicts with ADR 0019 B4 + the pending lint. So by-construction is the correct fix.

**Scope flag:** this adds a leaf to the `config.template` SSOT, which the live deployment reads — handling it as a dedicated block (config-SSOT changes are deployment-sensitive), not a rushed edit.

(Corrects my lane-claim's "`:memory:`-only" framing — incomplete for the file-DB specs.) — Grace

Its own comment names the exact #12435 risk: _"failed cleanup / a shared process / test-ordering means the next consumer reads the [mutated value]."_
- **The isolation helper is shipped:** `snapshotAiConfig` / `restoreAiConfig` (`test/.../memory-core/util.mjs`, via **#13315 / #13316**), used by-name in the ingestor setups.

So the safety-critical part (DB-path → live-bleed) is **lint-gated + green on dev** (CI), not merely helper-available. The 36 test files that touch `aiConfig` mostly mutate NON-DB-path leaves; a name-grep shows few using `snapshotAiConfig`, but the dangerous class is exactly the one the lint covers.

**Disposition:** #12435's safety-critical goal is ADDRESSED (lint + helper). The residual is lower-severity test-isolation hygiene for non-DB-path leaves (test-ordering flakiness, not live-bleed). **Close-eligible as filed** (the safety goal is met), or narrow to the hygiene residual — flagging for author/operator decision.

### @neo-opus-grace - 2026-06-29T16:04:10Z

**[V-B-A vs governing ADR-0019 — 2026-06-29]** This ticket's fix-shape (snapshot + restore) is **superseded by ADR-0019**, which was authored after #12435 and names it explicitly. Flagging before the ADR-violating shape gets implemented.

**ADR-0019 governs this exact ticket.** It lists #12435 as the live anti-pattern — row **B4 ⭐ SAFETY-CRITICAL**: *"runtime writes to `AiConfig` `[live: ~21 test files; #12435]`"* — and §4 names it the `#12335` orphan-bleed mechanism (~1,281 orphans `purgeTestCollections` reclaimed). The rule is unambiguous:
> *"a test NEVER mutates the shared `AiConfig`; isolation is by construction (`UNIT_TEST_MODE` → the config resolves the test DB). Never mutate the shared singleton (B4)."*

**Why snapshot/restore is the wrong shape — it is still mutation.** capture → mutate → restore-in-`finally` STILL writes the shared singleton during the test window. ADR-0019 §4 is explicit that the proxy set-trap routes `aiConfig.x = …` to `setData` on the owning provider (the shared singleton), so:
- a **concurrent / non-mutating read during the test** lands on test state (or a live read lands on the test DB), and
- a **failed cleanup** (the `finally` not reached — a throw, crash, or `--workers=1` ordering) leaves the live singleton corrupted.

Restore *narrows* but does not *close* the bleed window — the ADR's whole point is *no mutation*, not *mutation-then-restore*. The `DestructiveOperationGuard.spec` capture/restore precedent this ticket cites is itself a B4 instance the ADR supersedes.

**The ADR-sanctioned fix (the right shape):**
1. **Isolate by construction** — the ~21 specs resolve their test DB via `UNIT_TEST_MODE` (the config resolves test values by construction; mirrors the shipped `chromaTestIsolation`), removing the `aiConfig.<path> = …` writes entirely.
2. **ADR-0019 sub-#2 fail-build lint** — flag any `aiConfig.<path> = …` runtime assignment (especially in tests): *"one check prevents the whole orphan-bleeding class."* The mechanical enforcement the ADR prescribes (reviewer diligence is empirically insufficient — D1/E3).

**Recommendation:** re-scope #12435 to the ADR-0019-compliant shape (isolate-by-construction + the sub-#2 lint), OR close it as **superseded-by-ADR-0019** and track the work under the ADR's lint sub. Either way, **do not ship the snapshot/restore helper** — it entrenches the B4 anti-pattern with a wrapper.

(Candidly: I'm the reviewer who approved #12420 twice catching 0 of its 4 defects — the incident that *produced* ADR-0019. This is exactly the premise-over-execution lesson it codifies, so I'd rather catch the wrong-shape here than at merge.)

— Grace 🖖

### @neo-opus-grace - 2026-06-29T16:06:46Z

**[Update — the sub-#2 lint already ships]** V-B-A'd: the enforcement exists as `buildScripts/util/check-aiconfig-test-mutation.mjs` (wired into `lint-staged` on `test/**/*.mjs`). It's a **ratchet**: `DB_PATH_MUTATION` flags `aiConfig.(storagePaths|database|collections|logPath) = …` assignments (dot- or string-bracket-accessed, excluding `==`/`=>`/capture-reads), with an `ESCAPE_MARKER` (`aiconfig-mutation-ok`) + an **ALLOWLIST** of the grandfathered specs that "bites only NEW offenders; shrinks as the cleanup lands."

So the mechanical enforcement #12435 asked for is **already live** — new mutations fail-build; the ~21 grandfathered specs are the tracked ALLOWLIST. The only residual is the **by-construction migration** of those allowlisted specs (resolve the test DB via `UNIT_TEST_MODE`, drop the `aiConfig.x =` write, remove from the ALLOWLIST) — an on-touch cleanup the ratchet already governs, **not** a new snapshot/restore helper.

**Disposition (recommend):** **close #12435 as superseded-by-ADR-0019** — the ADR + the shipped ratchet-lint cover its intent, and the snapshot/restore fix-shape it proposes is ADR-violating. Let the ALLOWLIST shrink via on-touch by-construction migration (batch as files are touched — no standalone micro-PR). If you'd rather keep it open, narrow it to "migrate the ALLOWLIST specs to by-construction isolation." @tobiu — close vs narrow is your call. 🖖 — Grace

### @neo-opus-grace - 2026-06-29T17:24:35Z

## ADR-0019 V-B-A — the snapshot/restore approach is non-compliant; fix = per-test isolation — @neo-opus-grace

Grounding this lane against its read-gate (ADR-0019). The ~11 setups mutate the shared `AiConfig` singleton — e.g. `Orchestrator.spec.mjs`: `AiConfig.setData('orchestrator.devServer', {...})` and `AiConfig.openAiCompatible = {...}`, with hand-rolled save-then-restore. **ADR-0019 §2 is explicit: never mutate the SSOT.** So the ticket's proposed *'add snapshot/restore isolation'* would entrench the exact mutate-the-singleton antipattern the ADR forbids — and save/restore is itself a bug source: a test that throws before its restore leaks the mutation into every later test (the cross-test pollution this ticket names).

**Compliant fix direction:** don't mutate the global singleton in tests. Per ADR-0019, `AiConfig` is a hierarchical `Neo.state.Provider`; the isolation primitive is a **per-test child provider / isolated config the service reads from** — not mutate-and-restore the realm-root singleton.

**Re-scoping #12435:** 'add snapshot/restore' → 'replace singleton-mutation with ADR-0019-compliant per-test config isolation'. Flagging before anyone implements the non-compliant version. 🖖 — Grace

### @neo-opus-grace - 2026-07-02T06:46:14Z

## ✅ Resolved by a mechanical guard (stronger than the ~11-spec conversion this ticket proposed) — closing

V-B-A at current `dev`: the aiConfig-singleton-DB-path-mutation hazard this ticket flagged is now **mechanically prevented + CI-enforced**, and it guards the whole class ongoing rather than one-time-converting 11 specs:

- **`buildScripts/util/check-aiconfig-test-mutation.mjs`** — a lint that bans Class-A safety-critical DB-path leaf writes (`aiConfig.storagePaths.graph`, `.engines.chroma.database`, etc.) to the shared `AiConfig` singleton in tests — exactly the "orphan-bleed mechanism" this ticket describes (the reactive set-trap routes the assignment to the shared provider → test-ordering/shared-process bleeds the test DB into the next consumer, or a live read lands on test state). Tests must isolate by construction (`UNIT_TEST_MODE` resolves the test DB), never mutate the singleton. An `aiconfig-mutation-ok` escape marker exists for genuinely-unavoidable cases (judgment-call, not a blanket bypass).
- **CI-wired**: `package.json:241` + a dedicated workflow `.github/workflows/aiconfig-test-mutation-lint.yml`.
- **Currently clean**: ran it — **652 test files scanned, 0 new violations** (exit 0).
- **`snapshotAiConfig` util** (`test/.../util.snapshotAiConfig.spec.mjs`) exists for the controlled-mutation cases that need it.

**Why this supersedes the original plan:** the ticket proposed converting ~11 specs to snapshot/restore. A CI lint is the stronger shape — it catches the dangerous mutation-class in **all** current + future tests (652 scanned), not just the 11, so the hazard can't silently reappear. Same pattern as #14233 (resolved via a better mechanism than the ticket's proposed site). No residual. Closing as completed. 🖖 — Grace (assignee)
